title: Content-addressed manifests with exact replay and blocked overlap
description: Define stable identities and the first-release duplicate boundary while OQ-INGEST-4 lacks cross-file evidence.
decision: Define the Exact Replay key as Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version; filename is excluded. Derive batchId from that key and manifestRevisionId from canonical source-generated JSON. Derive sourceRecordId from Source Fingerprint, stable structural position, raw record-evidence fingerprint, and the immutable-facts schema while excluding adapter and manifest versions. Derive candidateId from selected account, sourceRecordId, and immutable normalized facts while excluding manifest revision and compatible version metadata; derive the LEDGER idempotency key and statement-import source reference from candidateId. Preview checks the complete Exact Replay key first and returns a prior active or completed batch without reimport only when every key component matches. An account or version change creates a new preview revision while unchanged financial facts retain stable candidate identities. The initial adapters claim no cross-file transaction identity. Identical tuples at distinct source positions remain distinct; a different source with an overlapping account/date window is blocked until identity-grade evidence resolves OQ-INGEST-4.
rationale: This fully satisfies safe v1 behavior without inventing identity. Tuple, fuzzy, and nearest-date matching can erase valid transactions and are disproven by the fixtures. Random IDs break deterministic replay. Persisting filenames as identity breaks rename safety and leaks private metadata. A content-addressed manifest binds approval and commit to exactly one reviewed meaning.
alternatives: A: random batch and candidate IDs, rejected because exact replay changes identity. B: filename or date-description-amount tuples, rejected because rename and duplicate tuples disprove uniqueness. C: fuzzy similarity suppression, rejected because false positives erase valid facts. D: content-addressed exact replay with overlap blocking, chosen.
consequences: Exact Replay is deterministic under any filename only when Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version all match. Account or version changes require a new preview rather than returning prior state, while version metadata remains excluded from source-record and candidate identities so unchanged financial facts do not become new candidates solely because compatible software changed. The initial adapters cannot auto-deduplicate across different sources; overlapping windows remain blocked until OQ-INGEST-4 gains identity-grade fixture evidence. Manifest canonicalization and hash compatibility remain explicitly versioned.
status_notes: Accepted fail-closed v1 design. Planning must create deterministic identity, manifest, replay, and overlap slices at these paths.
